([/ˈhʌf/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); born May 17, 1985[[1]](#cite_note-1)) is an American professional [Latin](/wiki/Latin_dance "Latin dance") and [ballroom dancer](/wiki/Ballroom_dance "Ballroom dance"), choreographer, actor, singer, and television personality.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) From 2007 to 2016, Hough was a professional dancer on the [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") dance competition series *[Dancing with the Stars](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(American_TV_series) "Dancing with the Stars (American TV series)")*, winning the show a record-breaking six times with his celebrity partners. For his work, Hough received 14 nominations for the [Primetime Emmy Awards for Outstanding Choreography](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Choreography "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Choreography"), winning the award 4 times. Hough later became a judge on the series beginning with its 29th season.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Fall2021-5)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American dancer, choreographer, actor, singer (born 1985)

| Derek Hough | |

**Derek Bruce Hough** ([/ˈhʌf/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); born May 17, 1985[[1]](#cite_note-1)) is an American professional [Latin](/wiki/Latin_dance "Latin dance") and [ballroom dancer](/wiki/Ballroom_dance "Ballroom dance"), choreographer, actor, singer, and television personality.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) From 2007 to 2016, Hough was a professional dancer on the [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") dance competition series *[Dancing with the Stars](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(American_TV_series) "Dancing with the Stars (American TV series)")*, winning the show a record-breaking six times with his celebrity partners. For his work, Hough received 14 nominations for the [Primetime Emmy Awards for Outstanding Choreography](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Choreography "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Choreography"), winning the award 4 times. Hough later became a judge on the series beginning with its 29th season.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Fall2021-5)

Hough has also appeared as an actor on stage, appearing at the West End premiere of *[Footloose: The Musical](/wiki/Footloose_(musical) "Footloose (musical)")* at the [Novello Theatre](/wiki/Novello_Theatre "Novello Theatre") and as well as the 2015 *New York Spring Spectacular* at [Radio City Music Hall](/wiki/Radio_City_Music_Hall "Radio City Music Hall") in New York City. In film and television, he has starred in the film *[Make Your Move](/wiki/Make_Your_Move_(film) "Make Your Move (film)")* and had a recurring role in the ABC musical-drama *[Nashville](/wiki/Nashville_(2012_TV_series) "Nashville (2012 TV series)")*. In 2016, he appeared as Corny Collins in NBC's live musical TV-production of *[Hairspray Live!](/wiki/Hairspray_Live! "Hairspray Live!")*[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From 2017 to 2020, Hough served as a judge on the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") dance competition series *[World of Dance](/wiki/World_of_Dance_(TV_series) "World of Dance (TV series)")*.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Since September 2025, Hough has been hosting *[Extra](/wiki/Extra_(American_TV_program) "Extra (American TV program)")* beginning with its 32nd season.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Hough grew up the fourth of five children in a [Latter-day Saint](/wiki/Latter-day_Saint "Latter-day Saint") family in the Salt Lake City suburb of [Sandy, Utah](/wiki/Sandy,_Utah "Sandy, Utah").[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) He is the son of Marianne and Bruce Hough, who was twice chairman of the [Utah Republican Party](/wiki/Utah_Republican_Party "Utah Republican Party") and ran for [Utah's 2nd congressional district](/wiki/Utah%27s_2nd_congressional_district "Utah's 2nd congressional district") in [2023](/wiki/2023_Utah%27s_2nd_congressional_district_special_election "2023 Utah's 2nd congressional district special election"),[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13) and has four sisters, Sharee, Marabeth, Katherine, and [Julianne](/wiki/Julianne_Hough "Julianne Hough").[[14]](#cite_note-family-14) All of his grandparents were dancers, and his parents met while on a ballroom dancing team in college in Idaho. He is the first cousin once removed of musicians [Riker](/wiki/Riker_Lynch "Riker Lynch"), [Rydel](/wiki/Rydel_Lynch "Rydel Lynch"), [Rocky](/wiki/Rocky_Lynch "Rocky Lynch"), Ryland, and [Ross Lynch](/wiki/Ross_Lynch "Ross Lynch") from the band [R5](/wiki/R5_(band) "R5 (band)"); their maternal grandmothers are sisters.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

When Hough was 12, his divorcing parents sent him to [London](/wiki/London "London") to live and study with dance coaches [Corky](/wiki/Corky_Ballas "Corky Ballas") and [Shirley Ballas](/wiki/Shirley_Ballas "Shirley Ballas"). Hough's sister Julianne joined him in London several months later. Originally intending to stay for three months, Hough remained in London for ten years (Julianne returned after five years). The Ballases helped tutor the two Hough children alongside their own son, [Mark Ballas](/wiki/Mark_Ballas "Mark Ballas"), schooling them at the [Italia Conti Academy of Theatre Arts](/wiki/Italia_Conti_Academy_of_Theatre_Arts "Italia Conti Academy of Theatre Arts"). They received training in singing, theater, gymnastics and many forms of dance, including jazz, ballet and tap. The three children formed their own pop music trio 2B1G ("2 Boys, 1 Girl") and performed at dance competitions in the UK and the U.S., and showcased UK television shows. Hough subsequently taught at the Italia Conti Academy of Theatre Arts.[[14]](#cite_note-family-14)[[17]](#cite_note-17) As well as being trained in many forms of dance, Hough can also play the piano, guitar, drums and bass.[[18]](#cite_note-theare.com-18)[[19]](#cite_note-DWTS_bio-19)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Competitive dancing and choreography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Competitive dancing and choreography")]

Hough won the IDSF World Latin U-21 Championship in 2002, and the [Blackpool U-21 Latin title](/wiki/Blackpool_Dance_Festival "Blackpool Dance Festival") with Aneta Piotrowska in 2003. He has won the LA Outstanding Dancer of the Year and New York Dance Alliance Outstanding Dance awards.[[19]](#cite_note-DWTS_bio-19)

On September 22, 2013, for the first time, the category for [Outstanding Choreography](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Choreography "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Choreography") for the [Emmy Awards](/wiki/Emmy_Awards "Emmy Awards") was moved to the [Primetime Live Show](/wiki/65th_Primetime_Emmy_Awards "65th Primetime Emmy Awards"), and all of the nominees in the category were to create and perform a number with the host of the show, [Neil Patrick Harris](/wiki/Neil_Patrick_Harris "Neil Patrick Harris"). Hough, who was nominated for *Dancing with the Stars*, was part of that number, along with the other nominees.[[20]](#cite_note-20) [Ken Ehrlich](/wiki/Ken_Ehrlich "Ken Ehrlich"), the executive producer of the show, stated, "It's definitely going to be an original number, something that hasn't been done before. It's going to utilize the talents of all of them to put this number together as choreographers."[[21]](#cite_note-21) Hough then went on to win the Emmy Award for Outstanding Choreography.[[22]](#cite_note-22) On September 12, 2015, Hough won his second Emmy Award for Outstanding Choreography, alongside Julianne Hough and Tessandra Chavez. This was both Julianne Hough's and Chavez's first Emmy win for outstanding choreography.[[23]](#cite_note-23) Including the two nominations that he went on to win, Hough has been nominated for nine Emmy Awards for choreography.

### Sochi 2014 Winter Olympics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Sochi 2014 Winter Olympics")]

In 2013, Hough worked with world champion ice dancers [Meryl Davis](/wiki/Meryl_Davis "Meryl Davis") and [Charlie White](/wiki/Charlie_White_(figure_skater) "Charlie White (figure skater)")[[24]](#cite_note-:6-24) on a number for their 2014 Sochi winter Olympics [short dance](/wiki/Short_dance "Short dance") program that had to contain [quickstep](/wiki/Quickstep "Quickstep") and [foxtrot](/wiki/Foxtrot "Foxtrot") rhythms.[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26) Davis and White went on to win the gold medal with the routine. Their win made Olympic history, marking the first Olympic title in the event for the United States.[[27]](#cite_note-27) About this opportunity, Hough said:

> I started working on a very special routine that's not for the show [*Dancing with the Stars*]. I'm choreographing a number for our world champion ice dancers Meryl Davis and Charlie White to perform at the Olympics next year. They graciously asked me to do it, and how could I say no? It's truly an honor, and they're amazing.[[24]](#cite_note-:6-24)

### Move Live On Tour

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Move Live On Tour")]

On March 18, 2014, Hough, along with his sister, Julianne, announced a summer tour of over 40 cities across the U.S. and Canada, called "Move Live on Tour", which would include dancing and singing from both of them, and the appearance of a group of dancers employed by the Houghs who were chosen through auditions.[[28]](#cite_note-28) They embarked on the sold-out tour on May 25, 2014, in [Park City, Kansas](/wiki/Park_City,_Kansas "Park City, Kansas") and ended it in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") on July 26, 2014. Due to the success of ticket sales and several sold-out venues before the tour had officially kicked off, several more shows were added to the tour schedule, which also sold out.[[29]](#cite_note-29) For the tour choreography, the Hough siblings collaborated with [Nappytabs](/wiki/Tabitha_and_Napoleon_D%27umo "Tabitha and Napoleon D'umo").[[30]](#cite_note-30)

Following the success of the 2014 tour and high demand, the Houghs announced the return of "Move Live on Tour" in the summer of 2015. Spanning from June 12, 2015, to August 8, 2015, the sold-out tour visited over 40 cities throughout the U.S. and Canada, and visited larger venues than in the previous year.[[31]](#cite_note-31) Tabitha and Napoleon D'umo "Nappytabs" returned as collaborating choreographers alongside the Hough siblings.[[32]](#cite_note-32) Auditions were also held to recruit a new group of dancers to join the Houghs, although some back-up dancers from the previous year returned.

On July 9, 2016, the Houghs held a free fitness pop-up event called 'Move Interactive' in Los Angeles, which they announced would be the first of many to come. The event started off in Sherman Oaks with a workout session at Pulse Fitness Studios led by celebrity personal trainer and owner, Mark Harari. The participants then took part in a 2-mile run along Ventura Blvd, before finishing off with a dance fitness class at JustDance Los Angeles. According to Hough, the motivation behind the event was to "bring health, love, community and human interaction into our everyday lives."[[33]](#cite_note-33) The following week, on July 14, the Houghs held a second free Move Interactive event in Fryman Canyon, LA, which included a hike and team building exercises.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

On December 14, 2016, they announced, via social media, that they would be going on a new tour, *MOVE BEYOND Live on Tour*, in 2017.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

### *Dancing with the Stars*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Dancing with the Stars")]

Hough made his first appearance on *Dancing with the Stars* in season four (week 6) as a guest instructor with Julianne and [Apolo Anton Ohno](/wiki/Apolo_Anton_Ohno "Apolo Anton Ohno"). He then joined the cast of professional instructors in [season five](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_5) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 5)") and was paired with [Jennie Garth](/wiki/Jennie_Garth "Jennie Garth").[[36]](#cite_note-36) Hough and Garth were eliminated in the semi-finals finishing the competition in fourth place.[[37]](#cite_note-37) For [season six](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_6) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 6)"), Hough was paired with actress [Shannon Elizabeth](/wiki/Shannon_Elizabeth "Shannon Elizabeth").[[38]](#cite_note-38) They were eliminated from the show on April 29, 2008, finishing the competition in sixth place.[[39]](#cite_note-39) For [season seven](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_7) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 7)"), Hough was partnered with model/TV host [Brooke Burke](/wiki/Brooke_Burke "Brooke Burke").[[40]](#cite_note-40) In rehearsal on October 4, 2008, Hough tripped and fell, hitting his head on the floor and momentarily blacked out. He was sent to the hospital, but sustained no injuries.[[41]](#cite_note-41) Hough and Burke earned the mirror ball trophy and won season seven on November 25, 2008.[[42]](#cite_note-42)

For [season eight](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_8) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 8)"), Hough was paired with rapper [Lil' Kim](/wiki/Lil%27_Kim "Lil' Kim").[[43]](#cite_note-43) They were eliminated on May 5, 2009, from the show finishing in fifth place.[[44]](#cite_note-44) For [season nine](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_9) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 9)"), Hough was partnered with model [Joanna Krupa](/wiki/Joanna_Krupa "Joanna Krupa").[[45]](#cite_note-45) They were eliminated during week nine, the semi-finals, on November 17, 2009, finishing fourth.[[46]](#cite_note-46) For [season ten](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_10) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 10)"), Hough was partnered with [Nicole Scherzinger](/wiki/Nicole_Scherzinger "Nicole Scherzinger"), the lead singer from the singing group [The Pussycat Dolls](/wiki/The_Pussycat_Dolls "The Pussycat Dolls") and former member of [Eden's Crush](/wiki/Eden%27s_Crush "Eden's Crush").[[47]](#cite_note-47) The couple reached the finals and won the competition on May 25, 2010.[[48]](#cite_note-48) That was the second win for Hough. For [season eleven](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_11) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 11)"), Hough won for the third time with actress [Jennifer Grey](/wiki/Jennifer_Grey "Jennifer Grey"), known for her film roles on *[Dirty Dancing](/wiki/Dirty_Dancing "Dirty Dancing")* and *[Ferris Bueller's Day Off](/wiki/Ferris_Bueller%27s_Day_Off "Ferris Bueller's Day Off"),* on November 23, 2010.[[49]](#cite_note-49) Hough did not participate in season 12 in order to be a part of the film *[COBU 3D](/wiki/Cobu_3D "Cobu 3D")* (later renamed to *Make your Move 3D*).[[50]](#cite_note-:0-50)

For [season 13](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_13) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 13)"), he was partnered with actress and TV host [Ricki Lake](/wiki/Ricki_Lake "Ricki Lake"), known for playing the original Tracy Turnblad in *[Hairspray](/wiki/Hairspray_(1988_film) "Hairspray (1988 film)")*.[[51]](#cite_note-51) Lake scored consistently high the whole season and ended up in third place.[[52]](#cite_note-52) For [season 14](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_14) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 14)"), Hough partnered with television personality [Maria Menounos](/wiki/Maria_Menounos "Maria Menounos").[[53]](#cite_note-53) In week seven, the couple earned the first 30 of the season and topped the leader board the first time in the season.[[54]](#cite_note-54) The couple made it to the semi-finals but were eliminated after they finished at the top of the leader board.[[55]](#cite_note-55) Menounos became the second celebrity in *Dancing with the Stars* history to be eliminated on a night where she came in first place with the judges (the first was [Willa Ford](/wiki/Willa_Ford "Willa Ford") in season three).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] For [season 15](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_15) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 15)") (*All Stars*), Hough was paired with season eight champion, [Shawn Johnson](/wiki/Shawn_Johnson "Shawn Johnson").[[56]](#cite_note-56) They were runners-up to [Melissa Rycroft](/wiki/Melissa_Rycroft "Melissa Rycroft") and [Tony Dovolani](/wiki/Tony_Dovolani "Tony Dovolani").[[57]](#cite_note-57)

On July 27, 2012, Hough announced that season 15 would be his last. A few months later, on February 22, 2013, Hough announced that he would be back for [season 16](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_16) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 16)"), stating on his Twitter account, "Wasn't sure if I was able to do this season because of my other projects, but worked it out so I can do both. #pumped.".[[58]](#cite_note-58) He was partnered with country singer and former *[American Idol](/wiki/American_Idol "American Idol")* contestant, [Kellie Pickler](/wiki/Kellie_Pickler "Kellie Pickler").[[59]](#cite_note-59) On May 21, 2013, Pickler was crowned season 16 champion, marking the fourth time Derek won the Mirror Ball trophy, and made him the only pro to have won four Mirror Balls.[[60]](#cite_note-60) On August 2, 2013, Hough confirmed that he would be back for [season 17](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_17) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 17)") via an interview with *[Access Hollywood](/wiki/Access_Hollywood "Access Hollywood")*.[[61]](#cite_note-Family_Dance_Off-61) The cast for season 17 was announced on September 4, 2013. Hough's partner for season 17 was *[Glee](/wiki/Glee_(TV_series) "Glee (TV series)")* star [Amber Riley](/wiki/Amber_Riley "Amber Riley").[[62]](#cite_note-62)[[63]](#cite_note-63) Riley was crowned season 17 champion, historically marking the fifth time Hough has won the Mirror Ball trophy, and made him once again the only pro to have won five Mirror Balls.[[64]](#cite_note-64)

For [season 18](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_18) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 18)"), Hough was paired with paralympic snowboarder, [Amy Purdy](/wiki/Amy_Purdy "Amy Purdy").[[65]](#cite_note-S18-65) In the finale on May 20, 2014, the couple was runner-up for the season, behind [Meryl Davis](/wiki/Meryl_Davis "Meryl Davis") and her partner [Maksim Chmerkovskiy](/wiki/Maksim_Chmerkovskiy "Maksim Chmerkovskiy").[[66]](#cite_note-66) On August 13, 2014, ABC announced that Hough would be back for the [season 19](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_19) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 19)")[[67]](#cite_note-67)[[68]](#cite_note-68) and in September, it was announced that he would be paired with [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") personality [Bethany Mota](/wiki/Bethany_Mota "Bethany Mota").[[69]](#cite_note-69) The couple made it to the first night of the finals, finishing in fourth place. On February 24, 2015, Hough was announced as one of the returning professional dancers on [season 20](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_20) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 20)") despite previously reporting he would not be taking part in this season. He was partnered with gymnast [Nastia Liukin](/wiki/Nastia_Liukin "Nastia Liukin").[[70]](#cite_note-70) They made it to the semi-finals, but were subsequently eliminated on May 12, finishing in fourth place.[[71]](#cite_note-71)

For [season 21](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_21) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 21)"), he was paired with wildlife conservationist [Bindi Irwin](/wiki/Bindi_Irwin "Bindi Irwin").[[72]](#cite_note-72) The couple made it to the finals and won the competition on November 24, 2015, marking Hough's sixth win on the show. Hough did not return for Season 22. On August 30, 2016, it was revealed that Hough would once again return as a professional dancer for [season 23](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(U.S._season_23) "Dancing with the Stars (U.S. season 23)"). He was paired with actress [Marilu Henner](/wiki/Marilu_Henner "Marilu Henner").[[73]](#cite_note-73) They were eliminated on November 7, 2016, finishing in 6th place which is tied for his lowest finish.

On September 8, 2020, it was revealed, after much speculation, that Hough would replace longtime DWTS judge [Len Goodman](/wiki/Len_Goodman "Len Goodman"). Due to the travel restrictions between England and the United States during the coronavirus pandemic, Goodman was unable to return for season 29.[[74]](#cite_note-74) He returned as a judge for its 30th and 31st seasons[[5]](#cite_note-Fall2021-5) and after the retirement and death of Goodman, he was named interim head judge for season 32.[[75]](#cite_note-75)

### *World of Dance*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: World of Dance")]

In May 2017, Hough joined NBC's new dance competition series *World of Dance*[[78]](#cite_note-78) as a judge, alongside [Jennifer Lopez](/wiki/Jennifer_Lopez "Jennifer Lopez") and [Ne-Yo](/wiki/Ne-Yo "Ne-Yo") and host/mentor [Jenna Dewan](/wiki/Jenna_Dewan "Jenna Dewan"). It was the top premiere of a new summer alternative series on broadcast in five years and the most-watched in nine years.[[79]](#cite_note-79) It was picked up for a second and continued to a third season and then a fourth.

### Theater

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Theater")]

Hough's earliest theater work include playing the lead in *[Jesus Christ Superstar](/wiki/Jesus_Christ_Superstar "Jesus Christ Superstar")* at the [Millfield Theatre](/wiki/Millfield_Theatre "Millfield Theatre"), and dancing in the company in *[Chitty Chitty Bang Bang](/wiki/Chitty_Chitty_Bang_Bang_(musical) "Chitty Chitty Bang Bang (musical)")* at the [London Palladium](/wiki/London_Palladium "London Palladium").[[80]](#cite_note-80)

In 2006, Hough starred as Ren in the original cast production of *[Footloose: The Musical](/wiki/Footloose_(musical) "Footloose (musical)")* at the Novelo Theatre in London's West End, as well as on the 2006 UK national tour.[[81]](#cite_note-81) Hough's West End performance earned him a nomination as The Stuart Phillips London Newcomer of the Year in the Whatsonstage.com Theatregoers' Choice Awards.[[82]](#cite_note-82) Hough made his Broadway debut on January 8, 2010, starring with [Kym Johnson](/wiki/Kym_Johnson "Kym Johnson") and [Mary Murphy](/wiki/Mary_Murphy_(choreographer) "Mary Murphy (choreographer)") in *Burn the Floor* for the final four performances of the show's Broadway run.[[83]](#cite_note-83)

On January 13, 2015, it was announced that Hough would appear in the *New York Spring Spectacular*, at [Radio City Music Hall](/wiki/Radio_City_Music_Hall "Radio City Music Hall").[[84]](#cite_note-84)[[85]](#cite_note-85) Due to the production, it was announced that Hough would not return for the 10th anniversary of *Dancing with the Stars*, however, during the cast announcement, Hough and partner [Nastia Liukin](/wiki/Nastia_Liukin "Nastia Liukin") were revealed as surprise contestants.[[86]](#cite_note-86)

### Acting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Acting")]

Hough made two cameo appearances; one in 2001 in the movie *[Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone](/wiki/Harry_Potter_and_the_Philosopher%27s_Stone_(film) "Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone (film)")*[[87]](#cite_note-87) and one in 2012 in the movie *[Rock of Ages](/wiki/Rock_of_Ages_(2012_film) "Rock of Ages (2012 film)")*, where his sister Julianne was playing the female lead role.[[88]](#cite_note-88) In 2011, Hough took one season break from *Dancing with the Stars* to star in his first movie *[Make Your Move](/wiki/Make_Your_Move_(film) "Make Your Move (film)")* (original title *Cobu 3D*)[[50]](#cite_note-:0-50) that premiered three years later, on March 31, 2014, in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles").[[89]](#cite_note-89)

On April 25, 2011, Hough made a guest appearance on the ABC show *[Better with You](/wiki/Better_with_You "Better with You")* as a dance instructor.[[90]](#cite_note-90) On August 14, 2014, it was announced that he would join the cast of the ABC series *[Nashville](/wiki/Nashville_(2012_TV_series) "Nashville (2012 TV series)")* for a multi-episode arc as Noah West.[[91]](#cite_note-91)[[92]](#cite_note-92) After three episodes during [season three](/wiki/Nashville_(season_3) "Nashville (season 3)"), Hough's character returns in the [fourth season](/wiki/Nashville_(season_4) "Nashville (season 4)") for more episodes.[[93]](#cite_note-93)

On April 18, 2016, Hough guest starred on [The CW](/wiki/The_CW "The CW") series, *[Jane the Virgin](/wiki/Jane_the_Virgin "Jane the Virgin")*.[[94]](#cite_note-94)

On April 27, 2016, it was announced that Hough would play Corny Collins in the [NBC live broadcast](/wiki/Hairspray_Live! "Hairspray Live!") of *[Hairspray](/wiki/Hairspray_(musical) "Hairspray (musical)")*, which aired on December 7.[[95]](#cite_note-95)

### Other media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Other media")]

Hough was one of [Bruno Tonioli](/wiki/Bruno_Tonioli "Bruno Tonioli")'s panelists on [BBC One's](/wiki/BBC_One "BBC One") *[DanceX](/wiki/DanceX "DanceX")*, the first episode of which aired on July 14, 2007.[[96]](#cite_note-BBC-96) He went on to choreograph and perform in [Cheryl Cole](/wiki/Cheryl_Cole "Cheryl Cole")'s "[Parachute](/wiki/Parachute_(Cheryl_Cole_song) "Parachute (Cheryl Cole song)")" music video, after being introduced to her by Tonioli.[[97]](#cite_note-97)

Hough was a founding member of the [Ballas Hough Band](/wiki/Ballas_Hough_Band "Ballas Hough Band") (formerly Almost Amy) and he shared lead vocals, played guitar and had several songwriting credits on the band's self-titled debut CD that was released by Hollywood Records in March 2009.[[98]](#cite_note-98)

Hough directed [Mark Ballas](/wiki/Mark_Ballas "Mark Ballas")' music video for his song "Get My Name" that was released on [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV") on May 14, 2014.[[99]](#cite_note-99)[[100]](#cite_note-100)[[101]](#cite_note-101)

Hough's first book *Taking the Lead: Lessons from a Life in Motion* came out on August 5, 2014[[102]](#cite_note-102)[[103]](#cite_note-103) and managed to enter [The New York Times Best Seller list](/wiki/The_New_York_Times_Best_Seller_list "The New York Times Best Seller list") on August 24, 2014.[[104]](#cite_note-104)

Along with Ballas, Hough bought a house in order to flip it for profit. The process of the home renovation was broadcast as a four-episode series on [HGTV](/wiki/HGTV "HGTV") called *Mark & Derek's Excellent Flip*. The show aired in the Spring of 2015.[[105]](#cite_note-105)[[106]](#cite_note-106)

In 2016, Hough appeared alongside [Lindsey Stirling](/wiki/Lindsey_Stirling "Lindsey Stirling") in the music video for *The Arena* which he also choreographed.[[107]](#cite_note-107)

In 2017, Hough participated in the celebrity edition of *[American Ninja Warrior](/wiki/American_Ninja_Warrior "American Ninja Warrior")* for [Red Nose Day](/wiki/Red_Nose_Day "Red Nose Day").[[108]](#cite_note-108)

In 2017, in an interview with *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")*, Hough revealed that he is going to be performing on the [alternative metal](/wiki/Alternative_metal "Alternative metal") band [Breaking Benjamin](/wiki/Breaking_Benjamin "Breaking Benjamin")'s upcoming album *[Ember](/wiki/Ember_(album) "Ember (album)")*, scheduled for a release in 2018.[[109]](#cite_note-109)

In September 2019, Hough hosted an NBC special Return to Downton Abbey: A Grand Event. In November 2019, Hough had emergency surgery to remove his appendix due to [appendicitis](/wiki/Appendicitis "Appendicitis"); he published a video of himself dancing with [Hayley Erbert](/wiki/Hayley_Erbert "Hayley Erbert") post-surgery.[[110]](#cite_note-110)

In September 2020, he signed a wide-ranging deal with [ABC Entertainment](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company").[[111]](#cite_note-111)

In September 2025, it was announced that Hough would replace [Billy Bush](/wiki/Billy_Bush "Billy Bush") as the host of *[Extra](/wiki/Extra_(American_TV_program) "Extra (American TV program)")* beginning with its 32nd season.[[112]](#cite_note-112)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Hough married fellow dancer [Hayley Erbert](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(American_TV_series) "Dancing with the Stars (American TV series)") on August 26, 2023 in [Monterey County](/wiki/Monterey_County,_California "Monterey County, California"), California.[[113]](#cite_note-113) They began dating in 2015 after meeting during the *Move Live Tour* *in* 2014.[[114]](#cite_note-114) In July 2025, the couple announced they were expecting their first child.[[115]](#cite_note-115) On December 29, 2025, Erbert and Hough welcomed a daughter who they named Everley Capri.[[116]](#cite_note-116)

## Awards and honors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Derek_Hough&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Awards and honors")]

Hough is a four-time Emmy winner for [Outstanding Choreography](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Choreography "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Choreography") and has earned fourteen [Emmy Award](/wiki/Emmy_Award "Emmy Award") nominations in total for his work on *[Dancing with the Stars](/wiki/Dancing_with_the_Stars_(American_TV_series) "Dancing with the Stars (American TV series)")*.

On April 10, 2014, Hough and his sister, Julianne, were honored at Mattel Children's Hospital [UCLA](/wiki/UCLA "UCLA")'s second annual Kaleidoscope Ball with the Kaleidoscope Award, an award that is given to people of the entertainment industry who bring light and laughter into the lives of children through their professional achievements and personal humanitarianism.[[123]](#cite_note-UCLA-123)[[124]](#cite_note-124)

On October 17, 2014, [GLSEN](/wiki/Gay,_Lesbian_and_Straight_Education_Network "Gay, Lesbian and Straight Education Network"), during its annual [Respect Awards](/wiki/GLSEN_Respect_Awards "GLSEN Respect Awards"), honored Hough with the year's Inspiration Award.[[125]](#cite_note-GLSEN-125)
